prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Alright, dear, imagine your computer or tablet as a busy little town. The CPU, or Central Processing Unit, is like the town's mayor. It tells all the parts of the computer what to do and when to do it. It’s really important because without it, the computer couldn’t work. It helps the computer do everything from playing games to browsing the internet. So, think of the CPU as the boss that keeps everything running smoothly!
